patch.value:
Sentry PM-CLI-27 (first seen 2026-07-12T07:47Z): "AbortError: Aborted with Ctrl+C" captured at error level from a dogfood host. A deliberate user interrupt is expected control flow, not a defect — capturing it as an unresolved error-level event pollutes the feed AND can block the daily auto-release sentry-telemetry-gate (same blocking mechanic as the GH-521/pm-wfvq torn-install episode, but this one is guaranteed-benign and user-triggered).

Fix direction: classify user-initiated aborts (SIGINT, readline/prompt AbortError) as handled before the Sentry capture layer — either skip capture or downgrade to breadcrumb/info with a distinct handled tag. Do NOT add a generic message pattern to the release gate's environment patterns (pm-wfvq rule: real regressions can look identical); the classification must happen at capture time where intent is knowable.

PM-CLI-27 itself resolved in Sentry as user-initiated after triage (this item owns the class fix).
Sentry captures deliberate Ctrl+C interrupts as error-level events (AbortError, PM-CLI-27)

user-initiated SIGINT/Ctrl+C aborts are classified as handled non-error telemetry (breadcrumb or info event, or not captured at all); AbortError from readline/prompt cancellation no longer creates unresolved error-level Sentry issues; sentry-telemetry-gate cannot be blocked by a user pressing Ctrl+C; regression spec covers the abort path

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Recurrence evidence 2026-07-12T15:36Z: this performance run interrupted an unintended interactive pm init prompt with Ctrl+C after pm test used schema context. Sentry correctly received PM-CLI-27 as handled=true but still reopened it at error/high; the linked runner was rerun successfully with --pm-context tracker. This is the same deliberate-abort class already owned here, not a new defect. Resolving the event group again pending the capture-time class fix.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Lineage backfill: parent under capability epic pm-5oj5 (zero-orphan convention, pm-doxj AC); directed PM-hygiene pass 2026-07-12

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Live recurrence evidence 2026-07-13: release gate reports PM-CLI-27 unresolved high with count=5 and last seen 2026-07-13T16:46Z. During this lane, two linked-test attempts with an unintended interactive TTY reached the pm init wizard and were deliberately cancelled with Ctrl+C, reproducing the already-owned handled AbortError class; the non-TTY tracker-context rerun completed. No duplicate item created or claimed.

author: codex-root

patch.value: Classify deliberate SIGINT and readline cancellations as handled control flow so user aborts do not create error-level Sentry issues or block release telemetry gates.

author: codex-root
message: Classify deliberate Ctrl+C AbortError as control flow before Sentry capture

value.note: User-initiated Ctrl+C and SIGINT capture policy

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Implementation evidence: Sentry capture now recognizes user-initiated AbortError Ctrl+C/SIGINT control flow without suppressing unrelated abort failures. Dedicated regressions and all exact-tree strict gates pass at 100/100/100/100 coverage.

patch.value: Classified only explicit user-initiated AbortError Ctrl+C/SIGINT paths as non-captured control flow while leaving unrelated abort errors observable.
Deliberate terminal cancellation does not create error-level Sentry issues or block the release gate.
Focused capture regressions pass, PM-CLI-27 is resolved, and the live reliability gate reports zero blocking highs.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Final verification: pm linked Sentry control-flow suite passes with sandbox parity; live PM-CLI-27 is resolved and the reliability gate reports zero blocking high/critical issues with healthy telemetry.

patch.value: Deliberate Ctrl+C control flow no longer creates Sentry errors; regression tests and live zero-high reliability gate pass.
